vif_chart.title: VIF Chart
description: The Variance Inflation Factor (VIF) chart visualizes the degree of multicollinearity
among the feature variables by displaying their respective VIF values. Higher VIF values
indicate stronger linear relationships with other predictors, which may lead to instability
in model coefficients and reduced interpretability. This chart enables quick identification
of problematic variables by highlighting those exceeding commonly accepted thresholds,
helping guide feature selection and dimensionality reduction decisions.

VIF Methodology

  1. Run Auxiliary Regression: Regress the predictor Xi against all other independent variables.
  2. Calculate R-Squared: Obtain the Ri² value from that regression.
  3. Apply VIF Formula:
    VIFi = 1 / (1 − Ri²)

acf_pacf_chart.title: ACF & PACF Chart
description: The ACF (Autocorrelation Function) and PACF (Partial Autocorrelation Function) plots
illustrate the correlation of each variable with its past values across different lag intervals.
These charts help identify temporal dependencies, seasonality, and potential lag effects within
the data. The inclusion of a smoothed trend line provides a clearer view of how correlations
decay or persist as the lag increases, aiding in the detection of significant lag structures.
This visualization is particularly useful for determining appropriate lag selections and
understanding the underlying time-series behavior of the variables.

ACF Methodology (Moving Average)

  1. Identify Total Correlation: Calculate the correlation between Yt and its lagged version Yt−k.
  2. Observe Decay: Check if the plot "tails off" (geometric decay) or "cuts off" abruptly.
  3. Determine MA(q) Order: The lag q where the ACF first drops into the [95% confidence interval]) defines the order.

PACF Methodology (Autoregressive)

  1. Isolate Direct Effect: Calculate the correlation between Yt and Yt−k while removing the influence of intermediate lags.
  2. Run Recursive Regressions: Solve the Yule-Walker equations or use OLS to find the partial coefficients φk,k.
  3. Determine AR(p) Order: The lag p after which the PACF identifies the autoregressive order.
